description: Severity scoring model for London civic issues. Final score = base_severity + sum(applicable modifiers). Scale: 1-10, where 10 = life-threatening emergency.

school_within_100m.rationale: Children are vulnerable; issues near schools pose elevated risk and public concern

hospital_within_200m.rationale: Hospitals serve vulnerable populations; access and safety are critical

care_home_within_100m.rationale: Elderly and disabled residents are more vulnerable to trip hazards, pollution, and noise

playground_within_50m.rationale: Children are directly exposed; drug paraphernalia, fly-tipping, and hazards near playgrounds are highest priority

busy_road_a_road_or_above.rationale: Higher traffic volume means more people affected and greater accident risk (DfT AADF data)

road_with_collision_history.rationale: Roads with STATS19 collision records in past 3 years indicate elevated risk

conservation_area.rationale: Planning and visual issues in conservation areas have stricter enforcement requirements

near_waterway_within_50m.rationale: Pollution and contamination risk to waterways; may require Environment Agency notification

2_reports_same_location_30_days.rationale: Multiple reports suggest the issue is persistent and unfixed

3_reports_same_area_30_days.rationale: Cluster of reports in same area (250m radius) suggests systemic problem

5_reports_same_area_30_days.rationale: Significant cluster; likely a hotspot requiring prioritised attention

previously_reported_and_unfixed_60_days.rationale: Issue was reported before but not resolved within standard SLA; council has failed to act

high_density_area.rationale: More people affected by the issue; Census 2021 LSOA population density data

very_high_density_area.rationale: Dense urban area (central London boroughs); significantly more people affected

asbestos_present.rationale: Asbestos is a serious carcinogen requiring specialist removal; do not disturb

needles_or_sharps.rationale: Immediate bloodborne infection risk; already reflected in base severity but compounds near schools

chemical_or_toxic_substance.rationale: Toxic substances require specialist response and may affect wider area

night_time_streetlight_fault.rationale: Streetlight faults are safety-critical after dark but irrelevant during daytime

night_time_noise.rationale: Noise at night (11pm-7am) has stricter regulations and greater impact on health

school_hours_nearby_school.rationale: Issues near schools during arrival/departure times (8-9am, 3-4pm) have elevated risk

blocking_road_completely.rationale: Complete road blockage affects emergency vehicle access and traffic flow

blocking_pavement.rationale: Pedestrians forced onto road; wheelchair and pushchair users particularly affected

blocking_emergency_access.rationale: Any issue preventing emergency vehicle access is critical

scoring_notes.cap: Final score is capped at 10. Scores above 10 from modifiers are clamped.
scoring_notes.emergency_threshold: Score >= 8 triggers emergency routing (999, gas emergency line, UK Power Networks emergency).
scoring_notes.urgent_threshold: Score >= 6 triggers urgent routing (24-48 hour expected response).
scoring_notes.standard_threshold: Score < 6 follows standard council SLA (typically 5-28 days).
